summary: PR 2377 linked `melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` by changing its configure entry from NonMatching to Matching, making a small source-shape adjustment in `ftCo_800BFFD0`, removing an include, and adjusting symbol metadata/local labels across data sections. The bot report records one new match for `main/melee/ft/ftcolanim` item `ftCo_800BFFD0`, raising it from 97.41% to 100.00%, with linked-code and linked-data gains.

key_files.role: Touched source for the matched ftcolanim unit; removed `ft/types.h` include and changed `ftCo_800BFFD0` local/argument expression shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists ftCo_800BFFD0 as new 100.00% match

key_files.role: Changed `melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` object classification from NonMatching to Matching.
Updated symbol metadata for local labels and data/sdata/sdata2 objects, including scope changes from global to local and address-style/local label names.

what_changed: `ftCo_800BFFD0` changed from computing `s32 temp_r7 = arg1 - 0x7B` and passing `temp_r7` to mutating `arg1` via `arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B)` with a new `long tmp`, then passing `arg1`.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` added local labels at `.data:0x803BAB60` and `.bss:0x80433318` and changed multiple symbol entries to `scope:local`.
Some symbols previously named as `ftCo_*` globals were changed to compiler-style local labels such as `@248`, `@249`, and `@250` at the same addresses.
The bot report records `ftCo_800BFFD0` as one new match, 97.41% before and 100.00% after, with +5 bytes for that item.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the code change for matching very narrow: one expression/local-shape change in `ftCo_800BFFD0`, one include deletion, and the configure flip.
The PR paired the source match with symbol metadata updates, including local scope annotations and local labels, rather than adding fake C storage in the source file.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small remaining mismatches, parameter reuse and local type/lifetime shape can be decisive, but should be kept narrow and tied to verifier output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800BFFD0 changed temp_r7 to arg1 mutation with long tmp
text_corpus: bot report records ftCo_800BFFD0 as new 100.00% match

decomp_lessons.lesson: When linking a unit, symbol metadata may need local-scope and local-label corrections alongside the source/configure change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt multiple scope:global to scope:local edits

naming_conventions.convention: Compiler-style local labels such as `@248`, `@249`, `@250`, and section labels like `...data.0` / `...bss.0` were used for local data metadata instead of semantic global names where the PR did not provide semantic ownership evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt replacements of ftCo_803C6AE8, ftCo_803C6B08, ftCo_804D3C10 with @248, @249, @250

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace a separate `s32 temp_r7 = arg1 - 0x7B` temporary with a `long tmp` and an in-place `arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B)` expression before calling `lb_800144C8`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new match for ftCo_800BFFD0
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence supports that this shape was part of the matching change, but does not include objdiff details explaining the exact register or instruction effect.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Flip the object status to Matching only after the function/unit report showed a new 100.00% match for `ftCo_800BFFD0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report is available, but no full build log or adjacent-function objdiff is present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: PR 2377 ftcolanim linked data scope local

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, dates, changed files
raw_diff: complete diff for changed files
changed_files: file list and line counts
text_corpus: PR body empty and bot report with match metrics
notes: There were no review comments and no human PR body text in the slice, so reviewer intent and author rationale are unavailable.
The bot report provides match-result evidence, but the slice does not include full objdiff, build logs, adjacent-function checks, or detailed assembly explanation.
Symbol metadata edits are directly visible, but the slice does not prove why each individual symbol scope/name change was required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftCo_800BFFD0 matched via parameter reuse/local lifetime shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2377, `ftCo_800BFFD0` changed from a separate `s32 temp_r7 = arg1 - 0x7B` temporary to a `long tmp` plus in-place `arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B)` before passing `arg1` to `lb_800144C8`; the bot report records this item as a new 100.00% match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftcolanim linked as Matching in PR 2377
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2377 changed `configure.py` so `melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` is listed as `Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftcolanim.c")`; the bot report lists one new match in `main/melee/ft/ftcolanim` for `ftCo_800BFFD0`.

source_update_candidates.title: Record ftcolanim matching status from PR 2377
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` was linked by changing its configure entry from NonMatching to Matching in PR 2377, with bot evidence of `ftCo_800BFFD0` reaching 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-specific status/update fact and should be curated into source-owned path facts only after review.

source_update_candidates.title: Narrow local-lifetime tactics need verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2377 is an example where a narrow local/parameter-shape change coincided with a new function match. It may be useful as an example under the existing matching-tactics standard, but the slice lacks detailed objdiff explaining the exact codegen mechanism.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be a supporting example for an existing standard, not a new accepted global rule.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that `arg1 -= (tmp = constant)` is preferred style; the PR only supports that this exact shape helped or accompanied matching for `ftCo_800BFFD0`.
Do not infer human review approval details; the slice contains no review comments.
Do not treat every `scope:global` to `scope:local` symbol edit as independently justified by the slice; the diff shows the edits, but not each symbol's ownership rationale.
